LIMITATION

The Plaintiff filed this suit before the District Court against the Defendant, invoking Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, as amended. The period prescribed for money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails is three years. The cause of action arises on the date of the failure; in this matter the date of failure is {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}}, which corresponds to {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant defaulted. The suit was instituted on {{FILING_DATE}} and is therefore within time since it is brought within three years of accrual.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

The Plaintiff values the subject matter of this suit at Rs. 20,00,000, being the amount paid as advance for the business transaction and sought to be recovered from the Defendant. This valuation is for the purposes of court fee and jurisdiction and is stated in the plaint filed before the District Court at {{DISTRICT_NAME}} on {{FILING_DATE}}.

The Court Fee payable on the plaint for a money recovery suit is computed as per the applicable State Court Fees Act, on ad valorem basis, i.e., at the rate of {{COURT_FEE_RATE}} on the suit valuation of Rs. 20,00,000, amounting to Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}; the proper court fee has been paid/affixed on this plaint.


INTEREST

Pre-suit interest: The Plaintiff advanced Rs. 2,000,000 to the Defendant on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} pursuant to the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}. The Defendant defaulted by not returning the amount on or before {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, and as of the filing of this suit on {{FILING_DATE}} the sum remains outstanding. Accordingly, the Plaintiff claims simple interest on Rs. 2,000,000 at the rate of 12% per annum from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} to {{FILING_DATE}}. This rate is justified as a commercially reasonable reflection of the cost of funds in short-term business lending, aligns with prevailing bank lending practices for similar commercial advances, and compensates the Plaintiff for the wrongful retention and use of the funds during the period of default, in light of the total failure of consideration alleged in this suit.

Pendente lite interest: During the pendency of this suit, the Plaintiff seeks pendente lite interest on the decretal sum of Rs. 2,000,000 at the rate of 12% per annum from {{FILING_DATE}} until the date of decree, under Section 34 CPC. Section 34 empowers the Court to award interest on money due on a contract at such rate as the Court may deem just, from the date of institution of suit until decree. The facts establish that the Plaintiff advanced Rs. 2,000,000 on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} and that the Defendant continued to withhold repayment from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} onward, thereby delaying the Plaintiff’s recovery of funds and necessitating relief by way of interest during the litigation to reflect the cost of funds and the harm caused by wrongful retention.

Future interest: Post-decree, from the date of decree until realization, the Plaintiff seeks interest on the decretal amount at 12% per annum simple interest, to be calculated until full realization. The legal basis for this post-decree interest rests on the Court’s general power under Section 34 CPC to award interest on the decretal sum at a rate the Court deems just, with such period extending from decree to payment, to secure the Plaintiff’s ultimate recovery of Rs. 2,000,000 and associated costs. The rate is justified for consistency with the pre-suit and pendente lite rationale and to provide effective relief in light of the Defendant’s wrongful retention of funds.

In sum, the Plaintiff requests pre-suit interest from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} to {{FILING_DATE}} at 12% per annum, pendente lite interest from {{FILING_DATE}} to decree at 12% per annum under Section 34 CPC, and future interest from decree until realization at 12% per annum, each calculated on Rs. 2,000,000, with the rate justified by the commercial character of the transaction, the Defendant’s wrongful retention, prevailing bank rates, and the absence of any contrary contractual rate.


PRAYER

(a) The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, prays that the Court may be pleased to pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff against {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} for Rs. 2,000,000 being the principal amount paid by the Plaintiff to the Defendant on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} pursuant to the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}, as a refund of the advance for a business transaction which failed due to the Defendant’s default, constituting total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. 

(b) The Plaintiff prays for pre-suit interest on Rs. 2,000,000 at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_RATE}} per annum from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} until {{FILING_DATE}} as compensation for the delay in return of the advance and the loss suffered by the Plaintiff, calculated as per the aforementioned rate for the stated period. 

(c) The Plaintiff prays for pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC on Rs. 2,000,000 at the rate of {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}} per annum from {{FILING_DATE}} until the date of decree on the amount claimed, being the amount due under the contract and already determined in favour of the Plaintiff. 

(d) The Plaintiff prays for future interest post-decree on the decreted amount at the rate of {{POST_DECREE_RATE}} per annum from the date of decree until realization, to be calculated and awarded as part of the final judgment. 

(e) The Plaintiff prays for costs of the suit amounting to Rs. {{COURT_COSTS}} including pre-litigation costs incurred for sending the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} amounting to Rs. {{PRE_LITIGATION_COST}} and the court fee paid on the plaint amounting to Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}, and for such other costs as the Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case. 

(f) The Plaintiff prays for such other relief, if any, as this Honourable Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case, including directions necessary to secure realization of the sum awarded. 

(g) General relief: In the alternative and without prejudice to the foregoing, grant such other and further relief as this Court may deem just and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case, in the interest of justice and equity, including any ancillary relief necessary to effectuate the aforesaid decrees and to secure full realization of the amounts awarded. 

Certified that the above prayers are consistent with the facts and law as pleaded in the accompanying sections of the plaint and are stated with the object of obtaining complete relief to the Plaintiff.
